contribution_description:
[CONTRIBUTION EVENT]
- Type: Time (Minutes)
- Amount: 20
- Description: Published @truesight_dao/dao-client to npm (scope rename + CI publish workflow)
  
  Pull requests (GitHub evidence):
  - https://github.com/TrueSightDAO/dao_protocol/pull/67
  
  Details:
  Got the browser DAO-client package published so the oracle/dapp can load it from a CDN. Diagnosed the publish blockers without ever exposing the token: the scope @truesight had no matching npm org (the org created was truesight_dao, account sophia_truesight as owner), so renamed the package @truesight/dao-client -> @truesight_dao/dao-client (package.json, README, src header); added a CI publish workflow (.github/workflows/npm-publish-dao-client.yml: workflow_dispatch + dao-client-v* tags, builds via prepublishOnly, npm publish --access public using the NPM_TOKEN repo secret) — dao_protocol PR #67. Set the NPM_TOKEN GitHub secret from the local .env via a subshell (token never printed). First publish failed E403 (the token wasn't a 2FA-bypass type); after the operator regenerated as an Automation/granular-write token, the workflow published @truesight_dao/dao-client@1.0.0. Verified live: npm registry latest=1.0.0, unpkg + jsDelivr both HTTP 200. Followed the new agent-attribution convention (Generated-by trailer). Oracle CDN re-wire intentionally deferred to a careful follow-up (verify-200 guard) rather than re-introducing the refactor that broke prod earlier.
- Contributor(s): Gary Teh
- TDG Issued: 33.33
- Attached Filename: N/A
- Destination Contribution File Location: N/A
